calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-08-2018 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Yulissa Zulaica, Esq. of Johnston Kinney & Zulaica LLP, T:415.693.0550, for defendant/moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT KATHLEEN MCDONAGH'S DEMURRER TO Annie O'Keefe's COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED on the statute-of-limitations ground. The one-year limitations period of CCP 366.2 applies in all of the factual/legal scenarios posited. First, it applies if Ms. McDonagh is viewed as a surviving spouse after her husband's death in August 2014 in a community property scenario. (Dawes v. Rich (1997) 60 Cal.App.4th 24, 34.) Second, it applies if Ms. McDonagh is viewed as a trustee. (Wagner v. Wagner (2008) 162 Cal.App.4th 249, 255; Levine v. Levine (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1265 (CCP 366.2 applies where no proceeding to administer decedent's estate and no notice to creditors).) Third, even were the complaint construed to establish Ms. McDonagh's acknowledgment of a debt in May 2015, the 366.2 statute ran in May 2016. All of this was before the petition for order confirming trust assets under Probate Code 17200 and 850 in September 2017 - and, again, before Ms. O'Keefe filed her complaint. Because Ms. O'Keefe has not shown how amendment could possibly cure her complaint's defect, the demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND.

ORDER SIGNED.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, email: mtorreano@sftc.org; torreanom@aol.com
